g.text: “Should one aspire for X ... then one should practice fully in virtue ... and practice in an empty dwelling.”
ākaṅkheyya ce … sīlesvevassa paripūrakārī … brūhetā suññāgārānaṁ
Each aspiration rests on the same five conditions:
May I be dear, agreeable, respected, and
worthy of veneration among spiritual companions
May I be a recipient of robes, alms food, lodgings,
and requisites for the sick, including medicines
May services offered by donors bring
May recollection by relatives with settled minds
May I be one who conquers discontent and delight;
may I repeatedly dwell overcoming any arisen discontent
May I be one who conquers fear and terror;
may fear and terror not overwhelm me;
may I dwell repeatedly overcoming any arisen fear and terror
Higher states of mind, pleasant abiding in the here and now
Not liable to states of suffering,
fixed in destiny, with full awakening as destination
Returns to this world only once,
then makes an end of suffering
May I, being one, become many;
touch moon and sun; reach Brahmā worlds
May I, with the divine ear element,
May I, discern the minds of other beings:
whether with passion or free from passion;
with aversion or free from aversion;
May I recollect manifold past lives:
one birth, two births, many births;
many cycles of contraction and expansion;
with names, clans, experience of pleasure and pain;
thus recall past lives with all aspects and details
May I, with the divine eye,
see beings passing away and being reborn,
understand how beings fare according to their kamma
Through the wearing away of the taints, realize in this very life
the taintless liberation of mind and liberation by wisdom
"Dwell being virtuous, accomplished in the moral code of conduct.
Dwell restrained by the restraint of this moral code of conduct,
mindful of proper behavior and keeping to suitable places,
seeing danger even in the slightest faults; train in the training guidelines."
